name: CalculateFacets
description: Obtain the facetted representation for the given chord-height tolerance.

parameters.Description:
Input Double that specifies the maximum distance that the facet can deviate from the smooth surface. This value is in centimeters. Smaller values can result in a much greater number of facets being returned and will require more processing time to calculate.

The picture below illustrates how the chordal tolerance is used when creating a stroked version of a curve. The chordal tolerance defines the maximum distance a line can be from the actual curve. When calculating facets the same concept applies but it defines the maximum distance between a triangle facet and the smooth surface.

The pictures below illustrate the difference obtained when calculating facets for a model using different chordal tolerances. The overall size of this model is approx. 6x6x4 cm’s. The picture on the left used a chordal tolerance of 0.1 cm and the one on the right used a tolerance of 0.01 cm.
parameters.Name: Tolerance

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of vertices that were generated. This is the number of vertices that will be returned by the VertexCoordinates argument.
parameters.Name: VertexCount

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of facets that were generated.
parameters.Name: FacetCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinate locations of the vertices. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent coordinates within the model space of the faceted object.
parameters.Name: VertexCoordinates

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that defines the normal for each vertex in the facet mesh. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent vector components within the model space of the faceted object.
parameters.Name: NormalVectors

parameters.Description: Output array of Longs that are used as index values to index into the vertex coordinate list. The first coordinate in the vertex coordinate list is index 1. Using vertex indices allows vertex coordinates to be reused by more than one triangle, thus reducing the overall resources required to define the entire mesh.
parameters.Name: VertexIndices

name: CalculateFacetsAndTextureMap
description: Obtain the facetted representation for the given chord-height tolerance. This also include the texture map coordinates.

parameters.Description:
Input Double that specifies the maximum distance that the facet can deviate from the smooth surface. This value is in centimeters. Smaller values can result in a much greater number of facets being returned and will require more processing time to calculate.

The picture below illustrates how the chordal tolerance is used when creating a stroked version of a curve. The chordal tolerance defines the maximum distance a line can be from the actual curve. When calculating facets the same concept applies but it defines the maximum distance between a triangle facet and the smooth surface.

The pictures below illustrate the difference obtained when calculating facets for a model using different chordal tolerances. The overall size of this model is approx. 6x6x4 cm’s. The picture on the left used a chordal tolerance of 0.1 cm and the one on the right used a tolerance of 0.01 cm.
parameters.Name: Tolerance

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of vertices that were generated. This is the number of vertices that will be returned by the VertexCoordinates argument.
parameters.Name: VertexCount

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of facets that were generated.
parameters.Name: FacetCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinates of the vertices.
parameters.Name: VertexCoordinates

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinate locations of the vertices. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent coordinates within the model space of the faceted object.
parameters.Name: NormalVectors

parameters.Description: Output array of Longs that are used as index values to index into the vertex coordinate list. The first coordinate in the vertex coordinate list is index 1. Using vertex indices allows vertex coordinates to be reused by more than one triangle, thus reducing the overall resources required to define the entire mesh.
parameters.Name: VertexIndices

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinates of the texture map.
parameters.Name: TextureCoordinates

name: CalculateFacetsWithOptions
description: Method that creates a new set of facets within the specified conditions.

parameters.Description: Input Double that specifies the maximum distance that the facet can deviate from the smooth surface. This value is in centimeters. Smaller values can result in a much greater number of facets being returned and will require more processing time to calculate.
parameters.Name: ChordalTolerance

parameters.Description:
Input NameValueMap that specifies additional tolerances that can be used to control the output mesh. The supported options are:

Name: MaxSideLength, Value: Defines the maximum side of any triangle in the mesh. A value of 0, or not supplying this value indicates that no maximum length is specified.

Name: MaxAspectRatio, Value: Defines the maximum length to height ratio that a triangle can have. This helps to avoid long skinny triangles. A value of 0, or not supplying this value indicates that no maximum aspect ratio is specified.

Name: MaxNormalDeviation, Value: Defines the maximum deviation between adjacent vertex normals. This value is the maximum angle allows between normals and is input as radians. A value of 0, or not supplying this value indicates that no normal deviation is specified.

Name: ConversionMode, Value: Specifies the conversion mode (0 or 2).

0 - Do not convert the display mesh to a Quad-Dominant mesh. This mode instructs the faceter to keep the generated display mesh (i.e., skip the tri-mesh to quad-dominant mesh conversion).
2 - Convert the triangular display mesh to a triangular mesh suitable for STL output, 3D printing or other similar application requiring a slightly higher quality triangular mesh.
parameters.Name: Options
parameters.undefined: 2 - Convert the triangular display mesh to a triangular mesh suitable for STL output, 3D printing or other similar application requiring a slightly higher quality triangular mesh.

parameters.Name: 0 - Do not convert the display mesh to a Quad-Dominant mesh. This mode instructs the faceter to keep the generated display mesh (i.e., skip the tri-mesh to quad-dominant mesh conversion).
2 - Convert the triangular display mesh to a triangular mesh suitable for STL output, 3D printing or other similar application requiring a slightly higher quality triangular mesh.

parameters.Description: Output Long that specifies the number of vertices in the facet mesh.
parameters.Name: VertexCount

parameters.Description: Output Long that specifies the number facets in the mesh.
parameters.Name: FacetCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinate locations of the vertices. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent coordinates within the model space of the faceted object.
parameters.Name: VertexCoordinates

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that defines the normal for each vertex in the facet mesh. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent vector components within the model space of the faceted object.
parameters.Name: NormalVectors

parameters.Description: Output array of Longs that are used as index values to index into the vertex coordinate list. The first coordinate in the vertex coordinate list is index 1. Using vertex indices allows vertex coordinates to be reused by more than one triangle, thus reducing the overall resources required to define the entire mesh.
parameters.Name: VertexIndices

parameters.Description:
Optional output array of Doubles that defines the texture coordinates that Inventor uses to map the render style texture to the face.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

name: GetExistingFacetTolerances
description: Method that gets the tolerances that were used to calculate the existing sets of display facets. These can be used to determine if any existing facets have been calculated within your desired accuracy. The tolerance value is also used as an index to specify which set of existing facets to retrieve when using the GetExistingFacets method. If you are using this method within Apprentice, you can use the DisplayAffinity property to optimize Apprentice for access to facets. Setting this property to True before you begin to traverse an assembly notifies Apprentice not to load any B-rep entities.

parameters.Description: Output Long that specifies the number of existing facet sets.
parameters.Name: ToleranceCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the tolerances that were used to compute the existing sets of display facets.

Optional input Boolean that specifies whether to get the 'leaf' source face in the case where there are multiple levels of override. This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

name: CalculateStrokes
description: Method that calculates the set of strokes that approximate all of the edges of the SurfaceBody, Face or Edge from which the method was called. The Tolerance argument defines the accuracy of the strokes. The output from this method represents a series of line segments, not connected polylines.

parameters.Description: Input Double that defines the accuracy of the strokes. This is a "chord height" tolerance. This is the maximum distance the stroke can be from the actual curve.
parameters.Name: Tolerance

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of vertices that were generated for the strokes. This is the number of vertices that will be returned by the VertexCoordinates argument.
parameters.Name: VertexCount

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of line segments represented by the strokes.
parameters.Name: SegmentCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinates of the vertices.
parameters.Name: VertexCoordinates

parameters.Description: Output array of Longs that are used to index into the vertex coordinates array. These indices provide the information that defines how the vertices are connected to create the strokes.
parameters.Name: VertexIndices

name: CalculateStrokesWithOptions
description: Method that creates a new set of strokes within the specified conditions.

parameters.Description: Input Double that specifies the maximum distance that the stroke can deviate from the smooth curve. This value is in centimeters. Smaller values can result in a much greater number of strokes being returned and will require more processing time to calculate.
parameters.Name: ChordalTolerance

parameters.Description:
Input NameValueMap that specifies additional tolerances that can be used to control the output strokes. The supported options are:

Name: MaxSideLength, Value: Defines the maximum side of any stroke in the polyline. A value of 0, or not supplying this value indicates that no maximum length is specified

Name: MaxNormalDeviation, Value: Defines the maximum deviation between adjacent vertex normals. This value is the maximum angle allowed between normals and is input as radians. A value of 0, or not supplying this value indicates that no normal deviation is supplied.
parameters.Name: Options

parameters.Description: Output Long that specifies the number of vertices used to define all of the output strokes.
parameters.Name: VertexCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Doubles that contains the coordinate locations of the vertices. The values are defined in x, y, z order within the array and represent coordinates within the model space of the stroked object.
parameters.Name: VertexCoordinates

parameters.Description: Output Long that specifies the number of polylines that the strokes represent.
parameters.Name: PolylineCount

parameters.Description: Output array of Longs that specifies the number of vertices that are used to define each polyline.
parameters.Name: PolylineLengths

name: Close
description: Closes this document.

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that specifies whether to skip the save. If SkipSave is set to True, it indicates that the changes to the document should not be saved and that the document should be closed silently. If SkipSave is set to False, it indicates that the normal save process should be followed (including prompting a dialog to the user). In addition, if Application.SilentOperation is set to True, the default choice on the dialog should be accepted. The Close method will fail if the combination of SkipSave = False and SilentOperation = True is used for a previously unsaved document.

This is an optional argument whose default value is False.
parameters.Name: SkipSave

name: GetSelectedObject
description: In order to provide better handling of system resources Inventor does not load all of the data when a document is opened but delays loading information until it is needed. A common need in many programs is to have the user select vertices on parts. Instead of loading the part model in order to return the true selected Vertex, Inventor returns a GenericObject. You can use this method to obtain more information about the selected object. If it is a vertex you can get the point coordinates without forcing the entire model to be loaded, or if you do need to do additional processing that requires access to the full model you can also force that by using the "SelectedObject" argument.

parameters.Description: Input object of type GenericObject that was obtained through a selection.
parameters.Name: Selection

parameters.Description: Output ObjectTypeEnum value that indicates the type of object selected.
parameters.Name: ObjectType

parameters.Description:
Output NameValueMap object that provides any additional information for the entity.  The following describes the data currently returned.
Object Type	Name	Value
Vertex	Point	Point
parameters.Name: AdditionalData

parameters.Description: Value
parameters.Name: Object Type

parameters.Description: Point
parameters.Name: Vertex

parameters.Description: Output ComponentOccurrence object that contains the entity that was selected.
parameters.Name: ContainingOccurrence

parameters.Description:
Optional output that returns the actual entity that was selected. If this argument is provided then Inventor will load whatever additional data is required from the referenced document in order to provide the entity.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: SelectedObject

name: Save2
description: Method that saves the document and the specified dependent documents.

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that specifies whether or not to save dependent documents that have been dirtied. Defaults to True indicating that the dependents will be saved. If the DependentsToSave argument is not specified, all dirty dependents are saved. If the argument is specified as False, only this document will be saved. The argument is ignored if the document doesn't have any dependents needing save.

This is an optional argument whose default value is True.
parameters.Name: SaveDependents

parameters.Description:
Optional input ObjectCollection that contains the Document objects to save. Use the Document.AllReferencedDocuments property to iterate over all dependent documents and find the ones that need to be saved (i.e. Dirty property returns True). If the SaveDependents argument is True and this argument is not specified, all dirty documents are saved. The argument is ignored if the document doesn't have any dependents needing save.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: DocumentsToSave

name: SaveAs
description: Saves this document into a file of the specified name.

parameters.Description: Input String that specifies the name of the file to save the document into.
parameters.Name: FileName

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that indicates whether the file to be saved is new or is a copy of a previously existing file.
parameters.Name: SaveCopyAs

name: SaveAs2
description: Saves this document into a file of the specified name.

parameters.Description: Input String value that specifies the full filename of the file to save the document into.
parameters.Name: FullFileName

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that indicates whether the file to be saved is new or is a copy of a previously existing file.
parameters.Name: SaveCopyAs

parameters.Description:
Optional input NameValueMap to specify additional options when save the document. Valid options include: Name = SaveDependents. Value = Boolean that specifies whether or not to save dependent documents that have been dirtied. Defaults to True indicating that the dependents will be saved. If the DependentsToSave argument is not specified, all dirty dependents are saved. If the argument is specified as False, only this document will be saved. The argument is ignored if the document doesn't have any dependents needing save. Name = DocumentsToSave. Value = ObjectCollection that contains the Document objects to save. Use the Document.AllReferencedDocuments property to iterate over all dependent documents and find the ones that need to be saved (i.e. Dirty property returns True). If the SaveDependents argument is True and this argument is not specified, all dirty documents are saved. The argument is ignored if the document doesn't have any dependents needing save.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: Options

If IgnoreErrors is set to False, the method returns as soon as the first error is encountered.

description: Property that returns the that is currently in edit.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentOccurrence.

name: AdjustProxyContext
description: Returns the specified object proxy scoped into this assembly, trimming any portion of the context from any assembly in which this assembly is a subassembly. In other words, for the given object proxy, this method makes a new object proxy which has this component definition as the context definition.

parameters.Description: An object representing an Object Proxy - known in earlier versions of Inventor as a Geom Proxy. Each Object Proxy has an associated ComponentOccurrence, which has a property called ContextDefinition. The value of this property is the top level ComponentDefinition.
parameters.Name: ObjectProxy

name: AnalyzeInterference
description: Method that analyzes the interference between two components. The input ObjectCollections contain the component occurrences that are to be checked for interference. If only one set is provided then interference checking is performed between all occurrences provided in the set. If two sets are provided then the overlap between the components of the two collections are calculated.

parameters.Description: Input that contains ComponentOccurrenceobjects. If this set is provided without Set2, all occurrences in the set are checked for interference against all other occurrences in the set.
parameters.Name: Set1

parameters.Description:
Optional input that contains ComponentOccurrence objects. When this set is provided the occurrences in Set1 are checked for interference against the occurrences in Set2.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: Set2

name: AppearanceOverridesObjects
description: Property that returns the objects that have appearance overrides in the active design view. The objects returned in the collection can include occurrences.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an ObjectsEnumerator.

name: Application
description: Returns the top-level parent application object. When used the context of Inventor, an Application object is returned. When used in the context of Apprentice, an ApprenticeServer object is returned.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

description: Property returning an AssemblyEvents collection object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AssemblyEvents.

name: AttributeSets
description: Property that returns the AttributeSets collection object associated with this object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AttributeSets.

property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a BOMStructureEnum.

description: Clears the appearance overrides on the provided objects. The objects can include faces, features, work surfaces, surface bodies and occurrences.

parameters.Description:
Optional input ObjectCollection including occurrences to clear their appearance overrides. If this argument is not provided, then it will clear all the appearance overrides in active design view. If an object is provided that does not have an override, it is ignored.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: AppearanceOverrideObjects

name: ClientGraphicsCollection
description: Property that returns the ClientGraphicsCollection object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ClientGraphicsCollection.

name: Constraints
description: Property that returns the AssemblyConstraints collection object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AssemblyConstraints.

name: CreateGeometryIntent
description: Method that creates a GeometryIntent object. GeometryIntent objects are used as input when creating assembly joints. They are used to identify geometry and specific locations on that geometry.

parameters.Description:
Input object that specifies the geometry.

Valid input objects are proxies of SketchLine, SketchCircle, SketchArc, SketchEllipse, SketchEllipticalArc, Edge, or Face objects.
parameters.Name: Geometry

parameters.Description:
Optional input that specifies a specific position on the geometry. This can be a value from PointIntentEnum, a Point, or a GeometryIntent object that specifies a position on the geometry relative to model space, a double value indicating a position in the parametric space of the input curve.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: Intent

name: CreateVisibleOccurrenceFinder
description: Method that creates an occurrence finder object that allows you to find all occurrences that are visible or hidden by a defined amount. By default, visible is defined by a portion of the part being visible from any view of the part. Optionally you can specify a camera to limit the viewing angle and the extents.

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that defines if visible or hidden objects should be found. If True then fully and partially visible occurrences will be returned. If False, completely and partially hidden occurrences will be returned.
parameters.Name: Visible

parameters.Description: Input Double that defines the percentage of the component that can be visible or hidden. A value of 1 (100%) indicates that all components that are visible at all, or that are completely hidden (depending on the Visible argument) will be returned. A value of 0 (0%) indicates that the most visible or hidden components will be returned.
parameters.Name: PercentageVisible

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that defines if all components that are found within an assembly will be consolidated so that their parent assembly is returned instead of each one of the child components. A value of True indicates that they will be consolidated.
parameters.Name: Compact

name: DataIO
description: Gets the object that directly deals with I/O to and from a storage-medium, including Streams(IStream).
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a DataIO.

name: DefaultVirtualComponentMaterial
description: Gets and sets the default material associated with the assembly component. This is the material that is used for newly created virtual components.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is an Asset.

name: Document
description: Property that returns the containing Document object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

name: ExportObjects
description: Method that marks all the input objects as exported.

parameters.Description: Collection of objects to mark as exported.
parameters.Name: Objects

name: FactoryDocument
description: Read-only property that returns the model state factory document when IsModelStateMember returns True.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Document.

name: Features
description: Gets the Features collection for this assembly (assembly features).
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Features.

name: FindUsingPoint
description: Method that finds all the entities of the specified type at the specified location.

parameters.Description: Input Point object that specifies the model space point at which to find the entities.
parameters.Name: Point

parameters.Description: Input array of SelelctionFilterEnum values that indicate the type of objects to find. The values are the enum values from the SelectionFilterEnum and can be combined to specify multiple object types.
parameters.Name: ObjectTypes

parameters.Description:
Input Double that specifies the tolerance value for the search. This value defines the radius of a sphere at the input point. All objects that intersect this sphere will be returned. If not specified, the default internal tolerance is used.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: ProximityTolerance

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that indicates whether or not invisible objects should be included in the search. Defaults to True indicating that invisible objects will be ignored.

This is an optional argument whose default value is True.
parameters.Name: VisibleObjectsOnly

name: FindUsingRay
description: Method that fires a ray through the part or assembly and returns the entities intersected by the ray. The objects intersected by the ray are returned in the order in which they are intersected, with the first entities returned being those closest to the clipping plane.

parameters.Description: Input object that defines the start point of the ray in model space.
parameters.Name: RayStartPoint

parameters.Description: Input object that defines the direction of the ray. The ray is infinite in the direction defined.
parameters.Name: RayDirection

parameters.Description: Input Double that defines the radius of the ray. This value is specified in centimeters. Defining a radius for a ray allows the intersection to be within a given tolerance. This radius essentially results in the definition of a cylinder whose axis lies along the axis defined by the RayStartPoint and RayDirection. The intersection result is between the cylinder and the model's entities. A value higher than zero is recommended, though not explicitly required.
parameters.Name: Radius

parameters.Description: Output that returns the entities that were intersected by the ray.
parameters.Name: FoundEntities

parameters.Description: Output that returns a set of Point objects. One Point object is returned for each entity in the FoundEntities list. The point defines the coordinate of the intersection between the ray and the corresponding entity.
parameters.Name: LocationPoints

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that specifies whether to find just the first entity in the ray's path. If False (the default) then all entities found are returned.

This is an optional argument whose default value is False.
parameters.Name: FindFirstOnly

name: FindUsingVector
description: Method that finds all the entities of the specified type along the specified vector using either a cylinder or cone that to define the tolerance within the defined vector.

parameters.Description: Input Point that defines the model space location to position the vector.
parameters.Name: OriginPoint

parameters.Description: Input UnitVector that defines direction to find all entities that the vector penetrates. The vector is treated as infinite in both directions from the origin point so all entities that lie in the path of the vector on either side of the origin point will be returned.
parameters.Name: Direction

parameters.Description: Input array of SelelctionFilterEnum values that indicate the type of objects to find. The values are the enum values from the SelectionFilterEnum and can be combined to specify multiple object types.
parameters.Name: ObjectTypes

parameters.Description:
Input argument that specifies if the vector defines a cylinder or cone when checking to see if an entity is hit by the ray.

This is an optional argument whose default value is True.
parameters.Name: UseCylinder

parameters.Description:
Optional input Double that specifies the tolerance value for the search. This value defines the radius of a cylinder if UseCylinder is True or the angle of the cone if False. If not specified, the default internal tolerance is used.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: ProximityTolerance

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that indicates whether or not invisible objects should be included in the search. Defaults to True indicating that invisible objects will be ignored.

This is an optional argument whose default value is True.
parameters.Name: VisibleObjectsOnly

parameters.Description:
Optional output that returns a set of Point objects. One Point object is returned for each entity in the FoundEntities list. The point defines the coordinate of the intersection between the vector and the corresponding entity.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: LocationPoints

name: GetEndOfFeaturesPosition
description: Gets the current end of Features position in the browser in an assembly.

parameters.Description: The assembly feature or sketch that the "End of Features" node is positioned immediately after. If the "End of Features" node is at the top of the tree this will return Nothing.
parameters.Name: After

parameters.Description: The assembly feature or sketch that the "End of Features" node is positioned immediately before. If the "End of Features" node is at the bottom of the tree this will return Nothing.
parameters.Name: Before

name: iMateResults

name: RepositionObject
description: Method that repositions the specifies object(s) to the new position within the collection of the object in the document.

parameters.Description: Input the Object that specifies the target object to move other objects next to. Valid object includes: PartFeature, ComponentOccurrence, Sketch, Sketch3D, WorkFeature.
parameters.Name: TargetObject

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that indicates whether to position other object(s) before or after the target object.  A value of True indicates that the object(s) will be positioned before the target object.
parameters.Name: Before

parameters.Description: Input Object that specifies the object to be repositioned.  Valid object includes: PartFeature, ComponentOccurrence, Sketch, Sketch3D, WorkFeature.
parameters.Name: StartObject

parameters.Description:
Optional input Object that specifies the object to be repositioned.  If specified, all the objects from the StartObject to the EndObject, both inclusive, will be repositioned to the specified position in the document.  If not specified, only the StartObject will be repositioned. Valid object includes: PartFeature, ComponentOccurrence, Sketch, Sketch3D, WorkFeature.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: EndObject

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that specifies whether the "End of Features" node is to be moved to the top or the bottom of the modeling portion of the browser. If True, the marker is moved to the top of the browser, just below the Origin folder. If False, it is moved to the bottom of the browser modeling section of the browser.

description: Method that process the property of a collection of occurrences.

parameters.Description: Input ObjectCollection that contains the ComponentOccurrence objects to set the grounded on. The collection can contain top-level occurrences only, if occurrences within a subassembly are specified then they will be ignored.
parameters.Name: pOccurrences

parameters.Description: Input String that indicates the property of the occurrences to set. Valid properties include: Grounded.
parameters.Name: PropertyName

parameters.Description: Input value to set for the occurrences for their property. Valid values for the properties are as below: PropertyName = “Grounded”. Value = Boolean that indicates whether the occurrences are grounded or not. A value of True indicates that their grounded will be turned on. The Occurrences collection can contain top-level occurrences only, if occurrences within a subassembly are specified then they will be ignored.
parameters.Name: Value

name: TransformOccurrences
description: Method that transforms a collection of occurrences.

parameters.Description: Input ObjectCollection that contains the ComponentOccurrence objects to be transformed. The collection can only contain top level occurrences from the document. An exception is the case of a flexible occurrence within the top level assembly, in which case, deeper level occurrences may be provided.
parameters.Name: Occurrences

parameters.Description: Input ObjectCollection that contains Matrix objects defining the transform for the input occurrences. The collection must have the same number of objects as the number of input occurrences, else the method returns an error. The collection can also contain a single Matrix, in which case all occurrences are transformed similarly using the single Matrix.
parameters.Name: Transforms

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean which specifies whether to transform the occurrences without re-applying the current assembly constraints. An update of the Assembly will honor the constraints and ignore these transforms. If not specified, a default value of False is used (i.e. constraints are honored).

This is an optional argument whose default value is False.
parameters.Name: IgnoreConstraints

name: CompatibleUnits
description: Method that evaluates the two input expressions and determines if the result in units that have the same base unit type.

parameters.Description: The first expression to be used in the comparison.
parameters.Name: Expression1

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the first expression is to be evaluated with. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch\: kInchLengthUnits and "in". String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in". A common use of this method will be to evaluate an expression using the current display units of the document. For example if the expression should define a length you can use kDefaultDisplayLengthUnit as the unit specifier so the expression will be evaluated as a length in the current document units. The enum values that specify to use the document default display units are kDefaultDisplayLengthUnit, kDefaultDisplayAngleUnit, kDefaultDisplayMassUnit, kDefaultDisplayTimeUnit, and kDefaultDisplayTemperatureUnit.
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier1

parameters.Description: The second expression to be used in the comparison.
parameters.Name: Expression2

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the second expression is to be evaluated with. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch\: kInchLengthUnits and "in". String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in". A common use of this method will be to evaluate an expression using the current display units of the document. For example if the expression should define a length you can use kDefaultDisplayLengthUnit as the unit specifier so the expression will be evaluated as a length in the current document units. The enum values that specify to use the document default display units are kDefaultDisplayLengthUnit, kDefaultDisplayAngleUnit, kDefaultDisplayMassUnit, kDefaultDisplayTimeUnit, and kDefaultDisplayTemperatureUnit.
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier2

name: ConvertUnits
description: Method that converts a value from one unit to another. The input and output unit specifiers must both define compatible units. For example, "in" (inches) and "cm" (centimeters) will work because they both define lengths. If incompatible units are specified, this method will fail. The converted value, in the units specified by the OutputUnitsSpecifier argument is returned.

parameters.Description: Input Double value that specifies the value to be converted. This value is in the units specified by the InputUnitsSpecifier argument.
parameters.Name: Value

parameters.Description: Input Variant value that specifies the unit type of the input value. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in". String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in".
parameters.Name: InputUnitsSpecifier

parameters.Description: Input Variant value that specifies the unit type of the output value. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in". String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in".
parameters.Name: OutputUnitsSpecifier

name: GetDatabaseUnitsFromExpression
description: Evaluates an input string and returns equivalent database units as a string.

parameters.Description: Input String value that contains the expression to evaluate. This string is evaluated the same ways a string entered in any of the Autodesk Inventor dialogs are evaluated. For example "3 in / 2 cm" is a valid string for input.
parameters.Name: Expression

parameters.Description: Input Variant value that specifies the unit type of the input value. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in". String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in".
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

name: GetDrivingParameters
description: Obtains the driving parameters enumerator corresponding to the input string.

parameters.Description: Input String value that contains the expression to evaluate. This string is evaluated the same ways a string entered in any of the Autodesk Inventor dialogs are evaluated. For example "3 in / 2 cm" is a valid string for input.
parameters.Name: Expression

name: GetLocaleCorrectedExpression
description: This method converts the input expression to a locale-friendly version. For instance, if you input "1.0 in" on a machine set to the German locale, the return value is "1,0 in". In this case, the decimal separator is different.

parameters.Description: Input String value that contains the expression to evaluate. This string is evaluated the same ways a string entered in any of the Autodesk Inventor dialogs are evaluated. For example "3 in / 2 cm" is a valid string for input.
parameters.Name: Expression

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the string is to be returned in. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and 'in'. string specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string 'in in in'.
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

name: GetPreciseStringFromValue
description: Obtains the precise string along with the units, given a value. The output units needs to be specified as well.

parameters.Description: Value from which to obtain precise string and units.
parameters.Name: Value

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the string is to be returned in. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in". string specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in".
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

name: GetStringFromType
description: Given a unit type from the UnitsTypeEnum as input, this method returns the string that can be used to specify the same unit type.

parameters.Description: Input constant value that specifies the unit type.
parameters.Name: UnitsType

name: GetStringFromValue
description: Method that creates a string that represents the input value evaluated using the specified units.

parameters.Description: Input Double value that contains the value in database units for the unit category specified in the second argument.
parameters.Name: Value

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the string is to be returned in. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in". string specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in".
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

name: GetTypeFromString
description: Given a string defining a unit this method returns the corresponding unit from the UnitsTypeEnum constant list. If the unit specified by the string does not exist in the constant list an error will occur. For example, inputting "mm" will return kMillimeterLengthUnits. This method is not currently supported when the UnitsOfMeasure object was obtained using Apprentice.

parameters.Description: Input string specifies the unit type.
parameters.Name: UnitsString

name: GetValueFromExpression
description: Method that evaluates the input expression using the units specified and returns a value in database units.

parameters.Description: Input String value that contains the expression to evaluate. This string is evaluated the same ways a string entered in any of the Autodesk Inventor dialogs are evaluated. For example "3 in / 2 cm" is a valid string for input.
parameters.Name: Expression

parameters.Description: Input variant value that specifies the unit type the expression is to be evaluated with. You specify a unit type using a value from UnitsTypeEnum or a string that describes a unit. For example, both of the following are valid unit specifiers for inch: kInchLengthUnits and "in." String specifiers are typically used for units that are not defined in the enum list. For example, the volume measure for cubic inches is not defined in the enum list but you can specify it using the string "in in in."
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

name: IsExpressionValid
description: Returns whether the input expression is valid or not.

parameters.Description: Input String value that specifies the equation. This string is evaluated the same way as strings entered in any of the Inventor dialogs are evaluated. For example '3 in + 2 cm' is a valid string for input.
parameters.Name: Expression

parameters.Description: Input value that specifies the type of unit the expression should be evaluated. The units can be specified using either a string defining a valid unit or an item from the UnitsTypeEnum. If the expression does specify a unit type, it should match the unit type specified by the UnitsSpecifier, otherwise an error will occur.
parameters.Name: UnitsSpecifier

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

name: GetAngle
description: Method that returns the angle between the input entities. The input entities must all belong to the same document, unless they are transient objects.

parameters.Description: Object that specifies the first entity. Valid inputs include Face, Edge, Vertex, transient objects (Point, Plane, Cylinder, etc.), work features, 2d and 3d sketch entities, DrawingViewCurve and GeometryIntent objects. Proxy object inputs are supported for assembly documents. For spherical and circular entity inputs, the center is assumed. For cylindrical and conical inputs, the axis is assumed. For toroidal inputs, the axis is assumed, unless the third argument of the method is also supplied in which case the center is assumed. For position inputs (such as Vertex, WorkPoint, Point, etc.) to be valid, all three inputs to the method should be supplied and they must all be position inputs (this implies a 3-point angle measurement).
parameters.Name: EntityOne

parameters.Description: Input object that specifies the second entity. Valid inputs include Face, Edge, Vertex, transient objects (Point, Plane, Cylinder, etc.), work features, 2d and 3d sketch entities, DrawingViewCurve and GeometryIntent objects. Proxy object inputs are supported for assembly documents. For spherical and circular entity inputs, the center is assumed. For cylindrical and conical inputs, the axis is assumed. For toroidal inputs, the axis is assumed, unless the third argument of the method is also supplied in which case the center is assumed. For position inputs (such as Vertex, WorkPoint, Point, etc.) to be valid, all three inputs to the method should be supplied and they must all be position inputs (this implies a 3-point angle measurement).
parameters.Name: EntityTwo

parameters.Description:
Optional input object that specifies the third entity for a 3-point angle measurement. Valid inputs include spherical & toroidal Face, Vertex, transient objects defining a position (Point, Sphere, etc.), work points, 2d & 3d sketch points, and GeometryIntent objects. Proxy object inputs are supported for assembly documents.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: EntityThree

parameters.Description: Input Document object that gets the measure tools angle precision from. Valid input includes PartDocument and AssemblyDocument.
parameters.Name: Document

name: GetLengthPrecision

parameters.Description: Input Document object that gets the measure tools length precision from. Valid input includes PartDocument and AssemblyDocument.
parameters.Name: Document

name: GetLoopLength
description: Method that returns the total length of a loop.

parameters.Description:
Object that specifies the loop. Valid inputs are:
An Edge object
A 2d/3d sketch curve (SketchLine, SketchArc, etc.) (For the above inputs, the method returns the length of the loop that the input entity is a part of (whether open or closed). Based on the input, the method looks for a unique closed loop or a unique open loop (in that order). If one is found, the length is returned else the method returns an error.)
An ObjectCollection containing Edge objects or 2d/3d sketch curve objects. (The collection can contain all the elements of a loop or must contain enough number of elements to uniquely identify a loop. Else the method returns an error. Also, the input curves must be end to end connected and in order.)
An EdgeLoop object
A ProfilePath object
A ProfilePath3D object
parameters.Name: Curves

name: GetMinimumDistance
description: Method that returns the minimum distance between the two input entities and also returns the closest points associated with both entities (not necessarily on the entity).

parameters.Description: Object that specifies the first entity.
parameters.Name: EntityOne

parameters.Description: Object that specifies the second entity.
parameters.Name: EntityTwo

parameters.Description:
Optional input InferredTypeEnum that specifies how the geometry of entity one is to be interpreted. Depending on the geometry of the entity one, different options are possible.
If entity one is a cylinder this can be either kNoInference (cylindrical surface) or kInferredLine (axis).
If entity one is a sphere this can be either kNoInference (spherical surface) or kInferredPoint (center).
If entity one is a cone this can be either kNoInference (conical surface) or kInferredLine (axis).
If entity one is a torus this can be either kInferredLine (axis) or kInferredPoint (center).
If entity one is a circular or elliptical curve this can be either kNoInference (the curve) or kInferredPoint (center).
For all other inputs, only kNoInference is valid.


This is an optional argument whose default value is 24833.
parameters.Name: EntityOneInferredType

parameters.Description:
Optional input InferredTypeEnum that specifies how the geometry of entity two is to be interpreted. Depending on the geometry of the entity two, different options are possible.
If entity two is a cylinder this can be either kNoInference (cylindrical surface) or kInferredLine (axis).
If entity two is a sphere this can be either kNoInference (spherical surface) or kInferredPoint (center).
If entity two is a cone this can be either kNoInference (conical surface) or kInferredLine (axis).
If entity two is a torus this can be either kInferredLine (axis) or kInferredPoint (center).
If entity two is a circular or elliptical curve this can be either kNoInference (the curve) or kInferredPoint (center).
For all other inputs, only kNoInference is valid.


This is an optional argument whose default value is 24833.
parameters.Name: EntityTwoInferredType

parameters.Description:
Optional output NameValueMap object that returns additional information regarding the measurement. Following are the possible return values (descriptions are below the table):
Name	Value Type
ClosestPointOne	Point object that returns a transient point closest to entity one in the minimum distance measure. This point may or may not lie on entity one (but will lie on the plane or axis of entity one). If entity one is a position input (Point, Vertex, WorkPoint, etc.), the position of the input entity is returned.
ClosestPointTwo	Point object that returns a transient point closest to entity two in the minimum distance measure. This point may or may not lie on entity two (but will lie on the plane or axis of entity two). If entity two is a position input (Point, Vertex, WorkPoint, etc.), the position of the input entity is returned.
ClosestEntityOne	Object type that returns the entity on which the returned ClosestPointOne lies. This is applicable only in the case where the input EntityOne is a ComponentOccurrence (or itG's proxy).
ClosestEntityTwo	Object type that returns the entity on which the returned ClosestPointTwo lies. This is applicable only in the case where the input EntityTwo is a ComponentOccurrence (or itG's proxy).
IntersectionFound	Boolean that indicates whether an intersection was found between entities one and two. If True, the method returns a value of 0.


This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: Context
parameters.undefined: Boolean that indicates whether an intersection was found between entities one and two. If True, the method returns a value of 0.

parameters.Name: ClosestPointOne
parameters.Type: Point object that returns a transient point closest to entity one in the minimum distance measure. This point may or may not lie on entity one (but will lie on the plane or axis of entity one). If entity one is a position input (Point, Vertex, WorkPoint, etc.), the position of the input entity is returned.

parameters.Name: ClosestPointTwo
parameters.Type: Point object that returns a transient point closest to entity two in the minimum distance measure. This point may or may not lie on entity two (but will lie on the plane or axis of entity two). If entity two is a position input (Point, Vertex, WorkPoint, etc.), the position of the input entity is returned.

parameters.Name: ClosestEntityOne
parameters.Type: Object type that returns the entity on which the returned ClosestPointOne lies. This is applicable only in the case where the input EntityOne is a ComponentOccurrence (or itG's proxy).

parameters.Name: ClosestEntityTwo
parameters.Type: Object type that returns the entity on which the returned ClosestPointTwo lies. This is applicable only in the case where the input EntityTwo is a ComponentOccurrence (or itG's proxy).

parameters.Name: IntersectionFound
parameters.Type: Boolean that indicates whether an intersection was found between entities one and two. If True, the method returns a value of 0.

name: Parent

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

name: SetAnglePrecision

parameters.Description: Input Long value that to set the measure tools angle precision. Valid value is from -1 to 8. When specify this to -1 the All Decimals is set.
parameters.Name: Precision

name: SetLengthPrecision
description: Set length precision.

parameters.Description: Input Document object that sets the measure tools length precision to. Valid input includes PartDocument and AssemblyDocument.
parameters.Name: Document

parameters.Description: Input Long value that sets the measure tools length precision. Valid value is from -1 to 8. When specify this to -1 the All Decimals is set.
parameters.Name: Precision

name: Type
description: Returns an ObjectTypeEnum indicating this object's type.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an ObjectTypeEnum.

name: ActiveModelState
description: Read-write property that gets and sets the name of the active model state for an assembly occurrence.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a String.

name: ActivePositionalRepresentation
description: Gets and sets the active Positional Representation for this occurrence.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a String.

name: Adaptive

property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: Appearance

property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is an Asset.

name: AppearanceSourceType
description: Gets and sets the source of the appearance for the component occurrence.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is an AppearanceSourceTypeEnum.

name: Application
description: Returns the top-level parent application object. When used the context of Inventor, an Application object is returned. When used in the context of Apprentice, an ApprenticeServer object is returned.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

name: AssociativeForeignFilename

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a String.

name: AttributeSets
description: Property that returns the AttributeSets collection object associated with this object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AttributeSets.

name: BOMStructure
description: Gets and sets the BOM structure override.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a BOMStructureEnum.

name: ChangeRowOfiAssemblyMember
description: Method that changes the row in the iAssemblyFactory this occurrence references. This method can be used only if the IsiAssemblyMember property returns True.

parameters.Description: Specifies the new row from the factory. The row index is specified either by a Long (row index), a String (MemberName of a member or DocumentName of a member), or an iAssemblyTableRow object.
parameters.Name: NewRow

parameters.Description:
Optional input NameValueMap object that specifies additional options for modifying the occurrence. This argument is currently ignored.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: Options

name: ChangeRowOfiPartMember
description: Method that changes the row in the iPartTable this iPartMember represents. This method can be used only if the iPartMember property is True.

parameters.Description: Specifies the new row from the factory. The row index is specified either by a Long (row index), a String (MemberName of a member or DocumentName of a member), or an iPartTableRow object.
parameters.Name: NewRow

parameters.Description:
Optional input array of Strings that specifies the input to use for the custom input. If the factory is a custom factory and this is not supplied the default values for custom values are used. The custom input strings are supplied in a column order. If the factory is not a custom factory this argument is ignored.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: CustomInput

name: Constraints
description: Property returns the collection of constraints that are acting on the occurrence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AssemblyConstraintsEnumerator.

name: ContactSet
description: Gets and sets whether the component belongs in the contact set.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: ContextDefinition
description: Property that returns the object this occurrence is in the context of. For example, all coordinate data returned by the definition is in the space of this ComponentDefinition.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentDefinition.

name: CreateGeometryProxy
description: Method that creates a proxy object for input object. A proxy object represents another object within the assembly space. Queries made on the proxy object are returned with respect to the assembly space, not the space the real geometry exists in.

parameters.Description: Input entity to create a proxy for. This entity must exist under the tree of this occurrence.
parameters.Name: Geometry

parameters.Description: Output proxy object created.
parameters.Name: Result

name: Definition
description: Property that returns the object this occurrence references.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentDefinition.

name: DefinitionDocumentType
description: Gets the type of the Document whose occurrence this is.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a DocumentTypeEnum.

name: Delete2
description: Deletes this component occurrence.

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean value to specify if skip save the source document of the occurrence. This default to False if not specified.

This is an optional argument whose default value is False.
parameters.Name: SkipDocumentSave

name: DisabledActionTypes
description: Gets and sets the action types valid for this component occurrence.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is an ActionTypeEnum.

name: Enabled
description: 'Put' is Inventor Only: Gets/Sets the Boolean flag that specifies whether this Occurrence is enabled or not.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: Excluded
description: Read-write property that gets and sets whether the occurrence is excluded in the active row of the iAssembly factory. This property returns False and cannot be set to True if the parent document is not an iAssembly.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: ExitEdit
description: Method that causes the component occurrence to return from the edit mode and into the environment specified by the input argument. If the ComponentOccurrence is not currently active (i.e. this is not the same occurrence as returned by AssemblyComponentDefinition.ActiveOccurrence), then this method does nothing.

parameters.Description: Input ExitTypeEnum that specifies the environment to exit to. Possible values are kExitToPrevious, kExitToParent and kExitToTop.
parameters.Name: ExitTo

name: Flexible
description: Only works for assembly occurrences. Gets and sets the Boolean flag that specifies whether the assembly is flexible.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: GetDegreesOfFreedom
description: Method that returns the available degrees of freedom for the occurrence.

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of available translational degrees of freedom. The value can be from 0 to 3.
parameters.Name: TranslationDegreesCount

parameters.Description: Output ObjectsEnumerator that returns Vector objects corresponding to the available translational degrees of freedom.
parameters.Name: TranslationDegreesVectors

parameters.Description: Output Long that returns the number of available rotational degrees of freedom. The value can be from 0 to 3.
parameters.Name: RotationDegreesCount

parameters.Description: Output ObjectsEnumerator that returns Vector objects corresponding to the available rotational degrees of freedom.
parameters.Name: RotationDegreesVectors

parameters.Description: Output Point object that returns the DOF center as displayed by Inventor.

name: GetDisplayMode
description: Gets the display mode type - default or override.

parameters.Description: Enum indicating the display mode.
parameters.Name: DisplayModeSourceType

name: GetReferenceKey
description: Method that generates and returns the reference key for this entity.

parameters.Description: Input/output array of Bytes that contains the reference key.
parameters.Name: ReferenceKey

parameters.Description:
Input Long that specifies the key context. The key context must be supplied when working with any B-Rep entities (and SurfaceBody, FaceShell, Face, Edge, EdgeUse and Vertex objects). A key context is created using the CreateKeyContext method of the ReferenceKeyManager object. For all other object types, the key context argument is not used and is ignored if provided.

This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.
parameters.Name: KeyContext

name: Grounded
description: 'Put' is Inventor Only: Gets/Sets the Boolean flag that specifies whether this Occurrence is grounded or not.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: HasAssociativeImportedComponent
description: Read-only property that returns whether the ComponentOccurrence has an associative imported component.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: HasBodyOverride
description: Property that returns a Boolean specifying whether the occurrence path contains body-modifying assembly features.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: iMateDefinitions
description: Property that returns the iMateDefinitions that are available from this occurrence. The iMateDefinition objects returned are actually proxies for the various iMateDefinition objects. These can be used as input to the Add and ValidResult methods of the iMateResults object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an iMateDefinitionsEnumerator.

name: ImportedComponent
description: Read-only property that returns the associative ImportedComponent object if the HasAssociativeImportedComponent returns True.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an ImportedComponent.

name: IsAssociativelyImported
description: Gets whether this occurrence is an associatively imported occurrence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: IsAssociativeToDesignViewRepresentation
description: Gets and sets whether this occurrence is associative to a design view representation.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

name: IsiAssemblyMember
description: Property that returns whether the ComponentOccurrence is an iAssemblyMember. The property returns True if the ComponentOccurrence is an iAssemblyMember. This property always returns False for occurrences of part documents.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: IsiPartMember
description: Property that specifies if the ComponentOccurence is an iPartMember. The property is True if the ComponentOccurence is an iPartMember.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: IsPatternElement
description: Property that indicates whether this occurrence represents a pattern element. In the case where this occurrence represents a pattern element, this property returns True. The PatternElement property can be used to get that pattern element.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: IsSubstituteOccurrence

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Boolean.

name: Joints
description: Inventor Only: Gets the enumerator that presents the joints applied on this Component Occurrence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AssemblyJointsEnumerator.

name: MassProperties
description: Property that returns the MassProperties object. This supports performing mass properties calculations for this particular occurrence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a MassProperties.

name: Name
description: Gets/Sets the human-readable name of this Component Occurrence.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a String.

name: OccurrencePath
description: Gets the path of immediate (directly instanced) occurrences that make up this occurrence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentOccurrencesEnumerator.

name: OccurrencePropertySets
description: Read-only property that returns a PropertySets object associated with the occurence.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a PropertySets.

name: OccurrencePropertySetsEnabled
description: Read-write property that gets and sets whether this occurrence has a PropertySets object.
property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Boolean.

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an OrientedBox.

property_value: This is a read/write property whose value is a Double.

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AssemblyComponentDefinition.

name: ParentOccurrence
description: Property that returns the parent of this occurrence. This property is only valid for the occurrences in a multi-level assembly that are not in the top level. The parent occurrence is the occurrence representing the subassembly this occurrence is contained within.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentOccurrence.

name: PatternElement
description: Property that returns the pattern element this occurrence represents. In the case where this occurrence is not part of a pattern this property returns Nothing. The IsPatternElement property can be used to check if this occurrence is part of a pattern.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an OccurrencePatternElement.

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Box.

name: RangeBox
description: Property that returns a Box object which contains the opposing points of a rectangular box that is guaranteed to enclose this object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a Box.

name: ReferencedDocumentDescriptor
description: Property that returns an enumeration of descriptors that represent the native document references held by this document.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a DocumentDescriptor.

name: Replace
description: Method that replaces the occurrence or all instances of an occurrence with another file.

parameters.Description: Input filename of the new file to be used to replace the existing occurrence.
parameters.Name: FileName

parameters.Description: Input Boolean that indicates whether the current occurrence should be replaced for all instances of this occurrence.

parameters.Description:

Optional input Boolean that specifies whether to keep the adaptivity after replacing the component(s). This defaults to False indicating the adaptivity is removed after replacement.

name: SetDesignViewRepresentation
description: Method that sets a design view representation for an assembly occurrence.

parameters.Description: This is an optional argument whose default value is "".
parameters.Name: Reserved

parameters.Description:
Optional input Boolean that indicates whether to associatively apply the design view. If set to True, any changes to the design view in the referenced assembly will show in this occurrence. If not specified, a value of False is used. This method fails if the input representation is a private design view representation and a value of True is specified for this argument (i.e. the associative option is invalid for private design views).

The display mode.

The collection returned provides access to the occurrences contained within the subassembly. If this collection is obtained from an occurrence that represents a part, it will not contain any occurrences.

The B-Rep queries will return coordinate data in the context of the component definition that served as the starting point to access the occurrence, which can also be accessed through the ContextDefinition property.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a SurfaceBodies.

name: Transformation
description: Property that returns and sets the transformation for the occurrence. The transformation matrix defines the position and orientation of the component within the assembly. When setting the transform of an occurrence the actual change in position and orientation of the occurrence is limited by the constraints on the occurrence.

name: Type

property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an ObjectTypeEnum.

name: AffectedOccurrenceOne
description: Read-only property that returns the first of the two occurrences affected by this joint. This is the same as the owning occurrence obtained from the OccurrenceOne property in the case where the owning assembly is not adaptive. Else, this is the first non-adaptive occurrence in the path leading from the owning occurrence to the occurrence that contains the first of the two geometries that this joint is between.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentOccurrence.

name: AffectedOccurrenceTwo
description: Read-only property that returns the second of the two occurrences affected by this joint. This is the same as the owning occurrence obtained from the OccurrenceTwo property in the case where the owning assembly is not adaptive. Else, this is the first non-adaptive occurrence in the path leading from the owning occurrence to the occurrence that contains the second of the two geometries that this joint is between.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is a ComponentOccurrence.

name: Application
description: Returns the top-level parent application object. When used the context of Inventor, an Application object is returned. When used in the context of Apprentice, an ApprenticeServer object is returned.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an Object.

name: AttributeSets
description: Property that returns the AttributeSets collection object associated with this object.
property_value: This is a read only property whose value is an AttributeSets.

name: Definition

name: GetReferenceKey
description: Method that generates and returns the reference key for this entity.

parameters.Description: Input/output array of Bytes that contains the reference key.
parameters.Name: ReferenceKey

parameters.Description:
Input Long that specifies the key context. The key context must be supplied when working with any B-Rep entities (and SurfaceBody, FaceShell, Face, Edge, EdgeUse and Vertex objects). A key context is created using the CreateKeyContext method of the ReferenceKeyManager object. For all other object types, the key context argument is not used and is ignored if provided.

This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.
parameters.Name: KeyContext

name: CreateAssemblyJointDefinition
description: Method that creates a new AssemblyJointDefinition object.

parameters.Description: Input AssemblyJointTypeEnum value that specifies the type of AssemblyJoint.
parameters.Name: JointType

parameters.Description:
Input GeometryIntent object that defines the first origin and origin direction of the connection on an occurrence that being connected with others. Valid geometries for defining the first origin and origin direction are:
Linear edge or sketch line on which the start/mid/end point can be specified as origin. The direction of the linear object defines the origin direction.
Circular/elliptical edge or sketch entity which the center will be used as origin. The normal of the object defines the origin direction.
Cylindrical/conical/elliptical-cylindrical face which the start/mid/end point on the axis of the face can be specified as origin. The axis of the face defines the origin direction.
Toroidal/spherical face which the center will be used as origin. The axis of the toroidal face defines the origin direction.
Planar face that has linear/circular/elliptical edges which the start/mid/end point of linear edge or center of circular/elliptical edge can be specified as origin. The normal of the planar face defines the origin direction.
parameters.Name: OriginOne

parameters.Description: Input GeometryIntent object that defines the second origin and origin direction of the connection on an occurrence that the first occurrence will be connected to. Refer to OriginOne for the valid geometries for defining the second origin and origin direction.
parameters.Name: OriginTwo

name: SetOriginOneAsOffset
description: Method that sets assembly joint origin as offset origin for OriginOne.

parameters.Description: Input Variant object to specify the offset value along the X-axis. A numeric or string value can be supplied for this argument and a parameter will be automatically created after the joint is created. If a numeric value is supplied, the value is in centimeters, and the parameter is set to the value specified. If a string value is supplied it will be used as the expression for the newly created parameter, if the value is supplied without a unit qualifier it will default to the current document length unit. The following is a valid entry for it, assuming the parameter d1 already exists and defines a length “d1+3 in”. If a geometry is provided, the offset value will be calculated with the OriginOne and the geometry. Valid geometries are Edge, Face, WorkPlane, WorkAxis and their proxies.
parameters.Name: XOffset

parameters.Description: Input Variant object to specify the offset value along the Y-axis. A numeric or string value can be supplied for this argument and a parameter will be automatically created after the joint is created. If a numeric value is supplied, the value is in centimeters, and the parameter is set to the value specified. If a string value is supplied it will be used as the expression for the newly created parameter, if the value is supplied without a unit qualifier it will default to the current document length unit. The following is a valid entry for it, assuming the parameter d1 already exists and defines a length “d1+3 in”. If a geometry is provided, the offset value will be calculated with the OriginOne and the geometry. Valid geometries are Edge, Face, WorkPlane, WorkAxis and their proxies.
parameters.Name: YOffset

name: AddAngleConstraint
description: Method that creates a new angle assembly constraint.

parameters.Description: Input object that defines the first object. This object can be a planar face, work plane, linear edge, work axis, or a face that defines an axis.
parameters.Name: EntityOne

parameters.Description: Input object that defines the second object. This object can be a planar face, work plane, linear edge, work axis, or a face that defines an axis.
parameters.Name: EntityTwo

parameters.Description: Input Variant that defines the angle between the two input entities. This can be either a numeric value or a string. A parameter for this value is created and the supplied string or value is assigned to the parameter. If a value is input, the units are radians. If a string is input, the units can be specified as part of the string or will default to the current angle units of the document.
parameters.Name: Angle

parameters.Description:
Optional input AngleConstraintSolutionTypeEnum that specifies the solution type. If specified to be kDirectionSolution, the solution always applies the right hand rule. If specified to be kUndirectionSolution, the solution allows either orientation, thus resolving situations where component orientation flips during a constraint drive or drag. If specified to be kReferenceVectorSolution, the ReferenceVectorEntity argument must be provided with a third entity for the solution. The default value is kDirectionSolution.

This is an optional argument whose default value is 78593.
parameters.Name: SolutionType

parameters.Description:
Optional input object that provides a third entity to solve the angle constraint. This argument is ignored if the SolutionType argument is not kReferenceVectorSolution. This object can be a planar face, work plane, linear edge, work axis, or a face that defines an axis.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: ReferenceVectorEntity

parameters.Description:
Optional input object that is used help in determining the initial position of the occurrence. The occurrences are repositioned in an attempt to make the two bias points coincident. This provides some general control over the position of the occurrence when it isn't being controlled by another constraint. An example of when the bias points are useful is the case when the first constraint on a part is a mate constraint. In the case where the mate is between two planes, the parts can be positioned anywhere along the infinite plane that defines their mating contact. Using the bias points you can define the position of the two occurrences, relative to each other. If a bias point is not given, one is calculated that is at the center of the parameter range of the input entity.

This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
parameters.Name: BiasPointOne

parameters.Description:
Optional input object that is used to help in determining the initial position of the occurrence. The occurrences are repositioned in an attempt to make the two bias points coincident.
